structure.name: Root
structure.description: Full-width block container, no background color (spec says dark bg always present, but Figma shows no bg on root - the event card itself IS the dark area)

children.name: Container
children.description: Max-width wrapper with 64px padding (uses container class)

children.name: Header
children.description: ThemeHeading area - eyebrow + heading + description in 2-column layout

children.name: Eyebrow
children.description: Roboto Medium 14px uppercase, dark text - editable

children.name: Heading
children.description: Anton 56px H3 style, dark text - editable

children.name: Description
children.description: Roboto Medium 16px body, dark text - editable

children.name: EventsList
children.description: Vertical stack of event cards (one or more), dynamically queried

children.name: EventCard
children.description: Single event row - two-panel card (image + content), 349px tall, both halves dark bg #1f1f1d with 25px border-radius

children.name: ImageHalf
children.description: Left 50%, dark bg, 16px padding, contains image with rounded corners (12px) and date badge overlay

children.name: Image
children.description: Event featured image, fills the area with object-cover, 12px border-radius

children.name: DateBadge
children.description: Floating badge top-right of image, dark bg #1f1f1d, 8px radius, shows day-of-week / day-number / month-year vertically stacked, 104px wide

children.name: ContentHalf
children.description: Right 50%, dark bg, 24px padding, content top + button bottom (justify-between)

children.name: ContentGroup
children.description: Top area with 8px gap between children

children.name: EventTitle
children.description: Anton Regular 48px/1.1, white - dynamic from post title

children.name: Location
children.description: General Sans Medium 16px/1.5, white - dynamic from event venue

children.name: EventDescription
children.description: Roboto Medium 16px/1.5, white - dynamic from post excerpt

children.name: Button
children.description: Text button 'VIEW EVENT' uppercase + arrow icon, anchored to bottom of content half - links to event post

possiblyOptional.reason: Standard ThemeHeading pattern allows optional eyebrow
possiblyOptional.visualEvidence: Present in Figma design but may not always be needed

possiblyOptional.reason: Standard ThemeHeading pattern allows optional description
possiblyOptional.visualEvidence: Present in Figma design but may not always be needed

possiblyOptional.reason: Not all events have a venue/location
possiblyOptional.visualEvidence: Shown in design but depends on event data availability

possiblyOptional.reason: Not all events may have an excerpt
possiblyOptional.visualEvidence: Shown in design but depends on event data availability

possiblyOptional.reason: Could be argued as always present for events, but may need to handle missing date gracefully

output.noInnerBlocksReason: This is a dynamic content block - event cards are rendered from PHP template (query results), not inner blocks. The event card is a repeating template part, not an authored inner block.

layoutRecommendations.approach: This block combines a ThemeHeading header (standard 2-column layout) with a dynamic events list below. No carousel is used - events stack vertically. The event card uses a side-by-side layout (image left, content right) similar to ContentWithMedia but with event-specific data.
layoutRecommendations.headerLayout: Use existing ThemeHeading component with headingSize 3 and columns 2, matching the dynamic-content-carousel pattern
layoutRecommendations.eventsListLayout: Vertical flex/grid stack for multiple event cards. Each event card is a flex row with two equal-width halves.
layoutRecommendations.eventCardLayout: Flex row with two children at 50% width each. Both halves have #1f1f1d background and 25px border-radius. Image half has 16px padding with inner rounded image. Content half has 24px padding with justify-between to push button to bottom.
layoutRecommendations.dateBadge: Absolutely positioned (or flexbox-aligned to top-right) badge overlaying the image. Dark background with rounded corners, stacked day-of-week/number/month-year text.
layoutRecommendations.responsive: At mobile, the two halves should likely stack vertically (image on top, content below). The 349px fixed height should become auto on mobile. The ThemeHeading should collapse to single column.
notes: The spec says 'dark background will always be present' - in the Figma design, the dark bg IS the event card panels (#1f1f1d), not a full-section dark background. The section itself has no background.
The two card halves sit flush against each other (no gap) but have separate border-radius, creating a split-card visual effect.
The Location field uses General Sans Medium font - this is different from the body font (Roboto). Planning agent should verify this font is available in the theme.
Multiple events stack vertically with presumably the same gap as header-to-card (64px), though only one event is shown in the Figma.
Button style matches the tertiary/text button pattern with an arrow icon.
The date badge floats over the image in the top-right corner within the inner rounded frame.

pendingQuestions.question: What is the vertical gap between multiple event cards when more than one event is displayed?
pendingQuestions.context: The Figma design only shows a single event card ('Single Event' frame name). The spec says multiple events stack vertically.
pendingQuestions.visualEvidence: Only one event card visible in Figma. Cannot determine inter-card spacing from this frame alone.
pendingQuestions.impact: Needed for the vertical stacking layout when multiple events are selected.

pendingQuestions.question: Is there a mobile breakpoint design for this block?
pendingQuestions.context: Only a desktop node (1215:17607) was provided for analysis. The frame is 1440px wide.
pendingQuestions.impact: Responsive behavior for the event card (stacking, date badge positioning, typography scaling) needs a mobile reference or design decision.

pendingQuestions.question: Should the section have a dark background behind everything (including the header), or is the dark background only the event card panels?
pendingQuestions.context: The spec says 'The dark background will always be present.' However, the Figma design shows the header text in dark color (#1f1f1d) on what appears to be a light/page background, with only the event card panels having the dark #1f1f1d background.
pendingQuestions.visualEvidence: In the Figma screenshot, the header area (eyebrow, heading, description) has dark text on a light background. The event card area has white text on #1f1f1d dark panels.
pendingQuestions.impact: If the entire section should have a dark background, the header text colors would need to be white instead of #1f1f1d. This contradicts the Figma design.
